mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in real personal savings rates indicates a demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint INDPRO-RSAFS composite and forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, rising housing starts amplify consumer wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when HOUST rises alongside GDP growth > 3% compared to baseline periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding productive capacity and healthy corporate throughput, which supports earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month INDPRO changes over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for highly leveraged firms, triggering margin compression and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining real personal savings rates signals a consumption-driven recession, which rapidly deteriorates corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-RSAFS composite fails to predict SPX direction with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase where GDP growth is accelerating but unemployment remains sticky above 4.5%, housing market strength acts as a leading indicator of consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive relationship with HOUST when GDPC1 growth exceeds 0.5% and UNRATE remains above 4.5%.
